mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that force immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction during documented labor market shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, confirming broad-based demand recovery that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of historical cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and inventory buildup, which compresses near-term corporate margins and triggers short-term negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns during tightening periods, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading exceeding 3.5% signals entrenched inflation that forces markets to anticipate restrictive monetary policy, increasing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5, or the signal loses predictive accuracy after 2022.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which sustains consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 60-day SPX returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates synchronized demand destruction, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to precede negative 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of observed quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which improves near-term corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when oil prices spike above $100 per barrel.
